title: biscutella laevigata subsp. tirolensis
intro: Biscutella laevigata subsp. tirolensis, also known as the Tyrolean biscutella, is a subspecies of the Biscutella laevigata plant, belonging to the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the European Alps, particularly in the Austrian and Italian regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Biscutella laevigata subsp. tirolensis are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and promoting digestion.
